steps.title: Extract points on f(x) and find the slope
steps.content:
Use $f(x) = g(x)(x+3)$ to find values of $f$:
  • At $x = -6$: $f(-6) = 0 \cdot (-3) = 0$ → point $(-6, 0)$
  • At $x = 9$: $f(9) = 5 \cdot 12 = 60$ → point $(9, 60)$

The slope is:
$$m = \frac{60 - 0}{9 - (-6)} = \frac{60}{15} = 4$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Using $f(x) = 4x + b$ and the point $(-6, 0)$:
$$0 = 4(-6) + b$$
$$b = 24$$

The $y$-intercept is $(0, 24)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
